# Key Figures — 1777

Notable individuals who shaped the year 1777.

<!-- BEGIN GENERATED: category-index — maintained by build-structure; do not edit by hand -->
| Topic | Description |
|---|---|
| [Benjamin Franklin in Paris](benjamin-franklin.md) | Elder statesman and diplomat who spent 1777 in Paris leading the commission whose negotiations turned Saratoga's victory into the formal French alliance of February 1778. |
| [Carl Friedrich Gauss](carl-friedrich-gauss.md) | Mathematician born April 30 1777 in Brunswick; later established foundational contributions to number theory, algebra, geometry, and physics. |
| [General George Washington](george-washington.md) | Commander-in-Chief of the Continental Army through the defeats of Brandywine and Germantown and the transformative winter at Valley Forge in 1777. |
| [General John Burgoyne](general-john-burgoyne.md) | British general who led the 1777 northern campaign and surrendered his army at Saratoga on October 17, 1777 — the turning point of the Revolutionary War. |
| [Lord George Germain](lord-george-germain.md) | Secretary of State for the Colonies (1775–1782) whose uncoordinated direction of the 1777 campaign left Burgoyne unsupported at Saratoga. |
| [Marquis de Lafayette](marquis-de-lafayette.md) | French aristocrat and volunteer major general who arrived June 13, 1777, was wounded at Brandywine, and became Washington's most trusted commander. |
| [Sir Henry Clinton](sir-henry-clinton.md) | Howe's second-in-command in 1777, passed over for the northern command and knighted in consolation; later British Commander-in-Chief in America (1778–1782). |
<!-- END GENERATED: category-index -->
